seo.title: FastAPI implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping FastAPI projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical roadmap for moving FastAPI applications from development to production. It focuses on async performance, security hardening, and specific patterns for high-concurrency workloads like LLM streaming.

items.title: Modularize Routes with APIRouter
items.description: Verify that all endpoints are grouped into logical modules using APIRouter and included in the main app via app.include_router() to prevent a monolithic main.py file.

items.title: Implement Pydantic BaseSettings
items.description: Ensure all configuration is managed through Pydantic's BaseSettings to enforce type validation on environment variables and provide default values.

items.title: Standardize Exception Handlers
items.description: Register global exception handlers for Starlette's HTTPException and Pydantic's ValidationError to ensure all error responses follow a consistent JSON schema.

items.title: Use Dependency Injection for Services
items.description: Validate that database sessions, third-party clients, and business logic services are injected using FastAPI's Depends() to facilitate mocking during unit tests.

items.title: Apply Strict Pydantic Types
items.description: Review all request/reponse models to ensure they use strict types (e.g., constr, PositiveInt) and Field constraints to prevent malformed data processing.

items.title: Configure Async Database Drivers
items.description: Verify the use of async-compatible drivers (e.g., asyncpg for PostgreSQL) and ensure every database interaction uses the 'await' keyword.

items.title: Optimize Connection Pooling
items.description: Set SQLAlchemy pool_size and max_overflow parameters based on the number of Uvicorn workers to prevent 'Too many connections' errors.

items.title: Automate Alembic Migrations
items.description: Confirm that database schema changes are strictly managed via Alembic versions and that migrations are executed as a pre-deployment step.

items.title: Eliminate Blocking Sync Calls
items.description: Audit the codebase for synchronous I/O libraries (like 'requests' or 'time.sleep') and replace them with 'httpx' or 'asyncio.sleep' to avoid stalling the event loop.

items.title: Implement Redis Caching
items.description: Apply Redis caching to high-latency read endpoints to reduce database load and improve response times for static or semi-static data.

items.title: Secure CORS Middleware
items.description: Restrict the 'allow_origins' list in CORSMiddleware to specific production domains instead of using wildcards.

items.title: Rotate JWT Secret Keys
items.description: Verify that JWT signing keys are managed via environment variables and use at least 256-bit entropy; ensure they are not committed to version control.

items.title: Set JWT Token Expiration
items.description: Confirm that all issued access tokens have an 'exp' claim set to a short duration (e.g., 15-60 minutes) to minimize the impact of token leakage.

items.title: Apply Rate Limiting
items.description: Implement rate limiting on sensitive routes (auth, password reset) using a library like slowapi or a Redis-backed counter to prevent brute-force attacks.

items.title: Disable Default Docs in Production
items.description: Set docs_url=None and redoc_url=None in the FastAPI constructor when running in production to prevent exposing the API specification to the public.

items.title: Use StreamingResponse for LLMs
items.description: Implement StreamingResponse for all LLM inference endpoints to return tokens as they are generated, preventing gateway timeouts for long completions.

items.title: Limit Concurrent Inference Calls
items.description: Use an asyncio.Semaphore to cap the number of simultaneous calls to external LLM providers or local models to manage cost and memory usage.

items.title: Implement HTTPX Connection Pooling
items.description: Initialize a single global httpx.AsyncClient instance to reuse TCP connections for external API calls, rather than creating a new client per request.

items.title: Configure Request Timeouts
items.description: Set explicit connect, read, and write timeouts on all external HTTP clients to prevent the application from hanging on unresponsive upstream AI services.

items.title: Validate Stream Termination
items.description: Ensure the application correctly handles client disconnects during a stream to immediately stop upstream inference and free up resources.

items.title: Configure Uvicorn Workers
items.description: Run Uvicorn via Gunicorn using the UvicornWorker class, setting the number of workers to (2 x CPU cores + 1) for optimal resource utilization.

items.title: Enable Proxy Headers
items.description: Include ProxyHeadersMiddleware if the app is behind Nginx or Traefik to ensure correct client IP and protocol detection.

items.title: Expose Health Check Endpoints
items.description: Create a /health route that verifies connectivity to the database and cache, used by orchestrators like Kubernetes for liveness/readiness probes.

items.title: Standardize JSON Logging
items.description: Configure the logging system to output structured JSON formatted logs to stdout for easier parsing by log aggregation tools.

items.title: Trace Requests with IDs
items.description: Implement middleware to inject a unique X-Request-ID into every request's context and include it in all logs for distributed tracing.
